SourceProxy.name: MotionDetector
SourceProxy.label: Motion Detector

Documentation.short_help: Detect motion points in lidar frame
Documentation.long_help: Motion detector to estimate motion probability of each points in frame and to extract clusters on motion points

SourceProxy.Documentation: Motion detector filter estimates motion probability of points in lidar frame by building a spherical depth map with gaussian mixture model. It also extracts clusters on motion points and gives the information of each cluster.

Property.name: Reset detection
Property.Documentation: Reset motion detector. The gaussian mixture model map is clear and parameters are reset to current state. The number of processed frames returns to zero.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Detection Range
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: To define the detection range. The motion detection is only applied on points that their distance from lidar is in the detection range [min, max].

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Azimuth resolution
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Resolution (in degrees) of the map to detect motion. For better results, this should be consistent with the LiDAR sensor resolution. But this may be increased for performance improvements at the cost of minimal object size detected.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Vertical resolution
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Resolution (in degrees) of the map to detect motion. For better results, this should be consistent with the LiDAR sensor resolution. But this may be increased for performance improvements at the cost of minimal object size detected.

IntVectorProperty.name: Initialization time
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: The number of frames to wait for the initialization of motion detector.

IntVectorProperty.name: Window size
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Duration (number of frames) during which a Gaussian will contribute to the overall mixture model. A shorter window size leads to a faster adaptation to changes in the underlying data distribution, making the model more sensitive to recent observations and noise. A longer window size results in a smoother temporal evolution of the model, making it less responsive to short-term fluctuations.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Subsample range
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Distance below which the subsampling of points will be activated

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Subsample resolution
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: If positive, resolution (in m) for close points subsampling (to avoid processing more points that necessary in high density scenarii) If negative, subsampling is disabled

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Removal outlier radius
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Radius used to remove isolated moving points for noise filtering

IntVectorProperty.name: Removal outlier neighbors
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: The number of neighbors that a point must have within the specified radius. Otherwise, the point will be removed.

IntVectorProperty.name: Cluster extractor
IntVectorProperty.Documentation:
Choose an extractor for clustering:
(0) Do not extract clusters
(1) Euclidean clustering: This method groups points based on their spatial proximity. It is ideal for identifying quickly compact motion areas.
(2) Gaussian mixture model: This method utilizes probabilistic model based on distances between points and works well in complex environments. It offers a rough tracking of clusters.
(3) Region growing: This method build a voxel grid on the motion points and expands regions in the grid. It is particularly useful when motion points are scattered but related. It offers a rough tracking of clusters.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Cluster radius
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: The search radius to find cluster.

IntVectorProperty.name: Cluster minimum point number
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: The number of neighbors that a point must have within the specified radius. Otherwise, the point will be removed.

IntVectorProperty.name: Set tracking window sizes
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Window size (in frames) to perform tracking in GMM. Higher values would result in objets that are tracked for longer (after disapearing)
